title: Quinn's Bedtime Feelings
synopsis: Quinn feels scared about bedtime until his diverse family helps him understand his feelings and find comfort through gentle routines and love.

pages.text: Quinn looked at his bedroom door. The sun was going down, and soon it would be bedtime. His tummy felt funny and his hands felt shaky. Quinn felt scared about going to sleep in the dark.

illustration.description: A small boy with curly brown hair and warm brown skin stands in a cozy living room, looking worried toward a hallway. Golden sunset light streams through windows. His posture shows nervousness with slightly hunched shoulders. Bright cartoon style with rounded shapes and warm colors.

pages.text: Mama noticed Quinn's sad face. She had kind eyes and wore her hair in a colorful headwrap. 'What's wrong, my little one?' she asked gently. Quinn's eyes filled with tears. 'I'm scared of bedtime,' he whispered.

illustration.description: A loving mother with dark skin and a beautiful patterned headwrap kneels down to Quinn's level, her expression gentle and caring. Quinn looks up at her with teary eyes. The room has warm lighting and soft textures like a construction paper background.

pages.text: Papa heard Quinn too. He had a big, warm smile and gentle hands. 'It's okay to feel scared sometimes,' Papa said. 'But we're here to help you feel safe and happy.' Quinn felt a little bit better knowing his family understood his feelings.

illustration.description: A tall father with light brown skin and a neat beard sits on the couch with Quinn and Mama. All three are close together in a family huddle. Papa's expression is understanding and warm. The scene shows bold primary colors and friendly, rounded shapes.
illustration.alt: Papa joining the family comfort session

pages.text: Mama and Papa walked with Quinn to his bedroom. They turned on his special night light that made soft, colorful shapes on the wall. 'Look how pretty and gentle the light is,' Mama said. Quinn started to feel less scared and more curious.

illustration.description: Quinn's bedroom with a magical night light projecting soft stars and moons on the walls in gentle blues and yellows. Quinn stands between his parents, looking more relaxed now. The room feels cozy with a textured background and warm, inviting colors.
illustration.alt: Quinn's bedroom with comforting night light

pages.text: Papa read Quinn's favorite story about a brave little bear. Quinn cuddled his soft teddy bear close to his chest. 'I can be brave like the bear,' Quinn said quietly. He was starting to feel proud and strong inside.

illustration.description: Quinn sits in bed holding a brown teddy bear while Papa, wearing glasses, reads from a colorful picture book. The bedtime scene is peaceful with soft pillows and blankets in cheerful colors. Everyone looks content and calm.
illustration.alt: Papa reading bedtime story to Quinn

pages.text: Mama gave Quinn gentle kisses and sang a quiet lullaby. Quinn felt warm and loved. His scared feelings became smaller, and his brave feelings grew bigger. 'Good night, sweet boy,' Mama whispered. Quinn smiled and felt happy and safe.

illustration.description: Quinn tucked in bed with a peaceful, sleepy smile, holding his teddy bear. Mama leans over to give him a goodnight kiss. The room is dimly lit with the night light creating a magical, secure atmosphere. Bold, soothing colors dominate the scene.
illustration.alt: Quinn feeling safe and happy at bedtime

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Quinn feel about bedtime at first?
questions.explanation: Quinn felt scared about going to sleep in the dark, which made his tummy feel funny and his hands shaky.

questions.question: What helped Quinn feel better in his bedroom?
questions.explanation: The special night light that made soft, colorful shapes on the wall helped Quinn feel less scared and more curious.

questions.question: What did Papa do to help Quinn?
questions.explanation: Papa read Quinn's favorite story about a brave little bear, which helped Quinn feel brave too.

questions.question: How did Quinn feel at the end?
questions.explanation: By the end, Quinn's scared feelings became smaller and his brave feelings grew bigger, making him feel happy and safe.

pages.text: Quinn felt _____ about bedtime, but his family helped him feel _____ and safe.
Mama and Papa used a special _____ and read a _____ to help Quinn feel better.
